title: URGENT: extend pss-document-service to render PO PDFs
description: Decision 2026-07-03: PO PDF generation moves into pss-document-service rather than a WeasyPrint sidecar or Node-side rendering. Port the legacy templates (app/templates/po_pdf.html + partials/certs_table.html, app/static/css/pdf_style.css, certs_table.json, base64 logo) into a doc-service endpoint the new app calls server-side. Output must stay visually identical to legacy WeasyPrint PDFs (paged-media page-X-of-Y footers). CROSS-REPO: the doc-service change is made in unison with the document-service chat/session — this bead tracks the purchase-order side and the coordination.

title: Dashboard: source projects from project_register, paginate, sticky 0005/0006
description: Test finding 2026-07-04 (Steve, item 1). Dashboard 'Purchase Orders by Project': (1) project list must come from project_register (canonical) instead of being derived from active_po_list project_id values, which surface deprecated project references; (2) paginate the table; (3) sort projects descending (highest project number first) EXCEPT 0005 and 0006 which are pinned to the top (sticky). PO counts still aggregate from active_po_list per legacy draft/non-draft rule. Note: projects appearing on POs but absent from project_register will no longer show — flagged to Steve.
close_reason: Dashboard reworked and verified in local container: projects sourced from project_register (register shows through 10380 vs 10168 max previously — deprecated-source symptom gone), 0005+0006 pinned top with amber tint, remainder descending, paginated 50/page (Page 1 of 6, ?page=2 link verified). Totals row covers all projects.

title: Phase 1 — read-only parity
description: Decision 2026-07-03: new app starts READ-ONLY against the shared production Supabase. Pages: dashboard (draft/active counts), PO list w/ filters (active_po_list view), PO web preview, expediting view (no editing), accounts view (no updates), spend report. Server-side data access via lazy getSupabaseAdmin() (SUPABASE_URL + SUPABASE_SECRET_KEY); all pages gated by Azure login (Supabase OAuth via vendored @platform/auth). Verify rendered data side-by-side against legacy UI.
notes:
All six read-only pages implemented and runtime-verified against live Supabase via dev server on 3017: dashboard (project summary + drill-down), po-list (~1000-row cap parity, status/project/supplier/date filters server-side), PO preview (007256-0006 format, sorted line items, accounting totals), expediting (50/page in-memory pagination, delivery flags, batched line-item fetch), accounts (offset-loop full fetch, completed/project/supplier filters, read-only inputs), spend report (12 months ending Jul 2026, drill-down links). Probes: bad uuid -> graceful alert; ?status=draft -> 11/11 draft; page=9999 clamps. Remaining before close: Steve's browser check (Azure login + visual parity vs legacy), ARM64 image build, Pi deploy, gateway registration commit.
Local test container running: docker-compose.local.yml (amd64 build on dev machine, purchase-order-local, healthy). Verified serving live data at http://localhost:3017/purchase-order/. Awaiting Steve's browser test incl. Azure login.

title: Register app with platform: PORTS.md + gateway proxy
description: platform-portal changes: add PORTS.md row (3017 | purchase-order | /purchase-order/), add production.conf redirect (location = /purchase-order -> 301 /purchase-order/) and proxy block (set $purchase_order_backend http://10.0.0.75:3017 — host-IP form used by all deployed standalones). Rebuild + push gateway image, up -d gateway on the Pi AFTER the app container is up.
notes: PORTS.md 3017 row + 3018 reserved, production.conf redirect + proxy block added in platform-portal working tree. NOT committed — tree already had unrelated uncommitted changes (incl. both these files); commit/push to be coordinated with Steve. Gateway rebuild+deploy pending until app deploys.

title: Scaffold Next.js app (port 3017, basePath /purchase-order)
description: Follow NEW_STANDALONE_APP.md: app/ with Next.js 16 + React 19, vendored packages/{auth,supabase,ui} from platform-portal (strip workspace:*, file:./packages/*), next.config.ts basePath /purchase-order + output standalone + transpilePackages, Dockerfile (multi-stage, non-root, EXPOSE 3000/PORT), app/.dockerignore (mandatory), docker-compose.app.yml (image ghcr.io/ukstevem/pss-purchase-order, container purchase-order, 3017, platform_net external, env_file ../platform-portal/.env), build.sh (buildx linux/arm64 --push). Copy conventions from pss-production-card (compose/build) + pss-matl-cert (app internals).
close_reason: Scaffold complete and pushed; next build green (Next 16.1.0/Turbopack). Vendored packages from production-card; Dockerfile/compose/build.sh at 3017; AuthGate + AppSidebar in place.

title: Scaffold repo: git init, gitignore, env example, beads, README
description: Bootstrap pss-purchase-order per pss-standalone-app skill: git init -b main + origin github.com/ukstevem/pss-purchase-order, .gitignore (secrets excluded), .env.example, NEW_STANDALONE_APP.md copied from platform-portal docs, bd init, README with identity block (port 3017, /purchase-order/, ghcr.io/ukstevem/pss-purchase-order).
close_reason: Scaffolded and pushed: commits 2b940e0 (bd init) + 38ea562 (gitignore/env.example/README/NEW_STANDALONE_APP.md) on origin/main. Local .env created from example (values still to fill from platform-portal/.env).

title: Rotate exposed secrets from legacy .env
description: Legacy .env (git-ignored but on disk) holds a live Supabase service-role key (SECRET_KEY, sb_secret_...), publishable key, and MS Graph client secret. SECRET_KEY is dual-purposed as both Flask session-signing secret and Supabase service-role key (app/__init__.py:22 vs supabase_client.py:20). Rotate all of these when the new system takes over; new app sources secrets from canonical platform-portal/.env. Do NOT rotate while legacy is live without updating its .env at the same moment.

title: Column-name mismatch: po_preview reads qty_recevied / exped_ccompleted_date
description: routes.py:249-250 (po_preview) reads misspelled columns qty_recevied and exped_ccompleted_date, while expediting.py:139-141,186 reads qty_received / exped_completed_date. Only one spelling can match the real Supabase schema, so either the web-preview expediting status or the expediting page is silently broken. VERIFY actual column names in Supabase before porting (live-DB query was not authorized during the survey). New app must use the confirmed spelling everywhere.
notes: VERIFIED during phase-1 runtime testing (2026-07-03): live po_line_items returns qty_received / exped_completed_date with real values — the correctly-spelled columns ARE the schema. Therefore legacy po_preview (routes.py:249-250) reading qty_recevied/exped_ccompleted_date is the silently-broken side: its delivered/partial statuses never fire from receipt data. New app uses correct names throughout (lib/po-logic.ts).

title: Migrate purchase order system to standalone Next.js app
description: Port github.com/ukstevem/purchase_order (Flask, C:/Dev/PSS/purchase_order) to the PSS standalone-app pattern: Next.js behind the platform-portal gateway, port 3017, route /purchase-order/, Azure login via Supabase OAuth. Legacy system stays live and UNMODIFIED; both run side by side on the same Supabase instance until proven. Feature parity target: dashboard, PO list, create/edit with revision snapshots, PDF generation + archive, Outlook draft on issue, expediting, accounts, spend report.

title: Port MS Graph Outlook-draft flow (Issue PO)
description: Decision 2026-07-03: included in parity. Server-side MSAL client-credentials -> create draft with PO PDF attached in shared purchasing mailbox (MS_TENANT_ID/MS_CLIENT_ID/MS_CLIENT_SECRET/MS_OUTLOOK_MAILBOX). Reimplement single-flight guard FAIL-CLOSED (legacy fails open — see pss-purchase-order-gcc.7). Beware duplicate drafts while both systems live: legacy keeps EMAIL_DRAFT_ON_PO active; new app must not issue the same PO.

title: Phase 2 — writes behind feature flag
description: After phase-1 verification: create/edit PO replicating the append-only revision-snapshot model EXACTLY (deactivate old po_metadata/po_line_items, insert new purchase_orders row, same DB-generated po_number, current_revision a,b,c->1,2,3, last_release stamping), expediting inline updates, accounts updates. Gate all writes behind a feature flag so read-only mode remains the deploy default until sign-off.

title: Legacy has zero auth and wide-open CORS
description: No login, no @login_required, no user identity; CORS(app) unrestricted (app/__init__.py:32); server uses the service-role key for everything, trusting only the network boundary. The new app must gate every page and API route behind Azure login (Supabase OAuth, client-side useAuth per ecosystem pattern) and keep the service-role key server-side only. Also feeds the RLS conversion task pss-purchase-order-hra.

title: advance_revision route uses non-existent 'revision' column
description: routes.py:1184-1219 (/po/<id>/advance-revision) and its helpers _get_po/_insert_po read and write a 'revision' column; the schema everywhere else uses 'current_revision'. Route appears legacy/dead and likely 400s or writes nothing. Decide during the port whether this endpoint is needed at all — the edit-PO flow already bumps revisions.

title: Convert Supabase schema to RLS once new app proven
description: Legacy app talks to Supabase with the service-role key and no RLS; tables are wide open to any anon-key holder. After pss-purchase-order is proven in side-by-side testing, add RLS policies to purchase_orders, po_metadata, po_line_items, suppliers, delivery_contacts, project_register(_items) and move the new app to authenticated access patterns. Blocked until parity + sign-off — the legacy app must keep working unmodified until cutover.

title: Legacy purchase_order findings — log only; fix after parity
description: Defects and code smells found in the legacy Flask system during the 2026-07-03 survey. Per agreed approach: DO NOT fix in the legacy repo and do not chase these during the initial port — replicate behaviour first, improve after the new system is running. Full survey: PSS-Vault/projects/pss-purchase-order/legacy-system-survey.md

title: Fragile CWD-relative asset paths in PDF generation
description: Logo and pdf_style.css are loaded as 'app/static/...' relative to the process CWD (routes.py:909,933,1024,1045) — only works because gunicorn runs from /app. In the new app resolve assets robustly (import-relative/bundled), and note the PDF pipeline choice is an open question (WeasyPrint sidecar vs doc-service vs headless Chrome).

title: Stale files/config in legacy repo (post-cutover cleanup only)
description: NO CHANGES while legacy is live. Catalogue: .flaskenv points at non-existent app.py (real entry run.py); debug=True hardcoded in run.py; FLASK_SECRET_KEY defined in .env but never read; empty 0-byte file named 'docker'; 'docker-compose.dev old.yml' (references lowercase dockerfileforpi — case-sensitivity trap); Dockerfile-old; templates/obsolete/ (4 dead templates); utils/project_filter.py queries non-existent 'projects_register' table and is never imported; utils/max_rev.py unused; manual delivery address code paths disabled but present.

title: Broad except-Exception swallowing hides errors
description: _to_date, _to_float, several fetchers, and _acquire_singleflight (fails OPEN — a lock error allows duplicate Outlook drafts) all swallow exceptions. New app: explicit error handling, surface failures, fail closed on the email single-flight.

title: Hardcoded VAT 20% and GBP currency
description: VAT computed as net_total * 0.2 in three places (routes.py:266,905,1021); currency hardcoded 'GBP' in utils/forms.py. Centralise both in config/constants in the new app (single source, still matching legacy output during parity testing).

title: Dead/duplicated code in supabase_client.py — do not port
description: supabase_client.py (~1075 lines) defines fetch_suppliers 3x (lines 132, 194, 402), fetch_projects 2x (153, 283), fetch_projects_map 2x (165, 375) — only the last definition wins. A second get_next_revision at line 912 collides with utils/revision.py's; routes use the utils one. Also unused-but-installed deps: flask_sqlalchemy (configured against sqlite that is never created), psycopg2-binary, supabase-py. Port only the live code paths.
